speaker: charlie
text: Right, now this next story sounds like something out of a sci-fi movie but it's absolutely real. <cite index="22-8">Scientists have developed 'click clotting' technology that can seal serious wounds in rats in seconds</cite>. We're talking about stopping potentially fatal bleeding faster than you can blink.

speaker: alice
text: The implications are mind-blowing Alex! Think about emergency rooms, battlefield medicine, even everyday accidents. <cite index="21-20,21-21">Plus there's another breakthrough where scientists are making major progress toward freezing organs for future transplants without damaging them - they've figured out how to prevent cracking during ultra-cold preservation</cite>.

speaker: charlie
text: <cite index="28-26,28-27">And if that wasn't enough, engineers have printed artificial neurons that can actually communicate with living brain cells</cite>. The line between biology and technology is getting properly blurred, and it's all happening to help people live better lives.
